title: Real Estate
summary: To ensure our employees and customers experience a coherent expression of the SEB brand across all of our branches and buildings worldwide, we have created this interior design guideline.

columns.text: To ensure our employees and customers experience a coherent expression of the SEB brand across all of our branches and buildings worldwide, we have created this interior design guideline.
It sets the standard for all future interior design projects and provides guidance for design decisions including the use of materials, color, furniture, and lighting.
Our interior expression is based on the following three key design principles. They should be considered at all times when making interior design decisions.
The overall expression should be one of simplicity. The whole environment needs to be guided by this principle.Simplicity of formBasic geometric shapes inform the design of our furniture and signage.
Simplicity of colourComplementary colour combinations. Not too many colours used at once.
Simplicity of patternWallpaper and upholstery patterns are geometric.
Asymmetric arrangementWe position our furniture in interesting and unexpected groupings. Asymmetric placement of carpets can help to create zones, or ‘rooms within a room’.
Asymmetric color balanceMix dark/neutral furniture with small spots of colored furniture to achieve an asymmetric balance of color.
Dynamic use of spaceCreate flexible spaces with multiple usages.
Dynamic use of colorUse bold accents of color to create visual interest and define spaces.
We have a number of tools that we use to create the SEB interior experience.
The choice and combination of materials is key to achieving the right feel in our interior environments. We use authentic, high quality and long lasting materials to create the right impression.
Primary wood choiceOur primary recommended wood color is White Ash (vitpigmenterad ask) for tables and panels within our interior environments.

ContrastWe can also make use of Black Ash and Oak wood for additional contrast. Consideration must be given to the colors/materials used on the floor and walls. Always aim for a nice contrast of colors – for instance, if the room has stone floors and grey walls, do not use a grey table.
Our wooden slatted panels are a signature item to drive recognition of the SEB brand anywhere in the world.

They have three functions:1. To provide a recognizable brand element in interior environments2. For functional purposes, such as framing a digital screen or housing an area for customers to fill out forms and documentation.3. For acoustics, to help reduce noise.
As every space is different, the application & purpose will need to be determined in each individual case.
Color is an immediate tool to signal our brand. It helps us to define spaces, draw attention to specific areas and allows us to create different moods with an interior environment.

Color useIt’s important to consider the appropriateness of color choice for each area of the building or interior experience. For instance, an office environment may need to be calm and neutral in color, whereas interior signage would need to be much brighter to capture attention. Please don’t overuse the color black, and keep in mind that black isn’t always a good color, as it can look worn.
ContrastAs an overall expression, it’s important to have variation and contrast of bright, neutral, and monochrome colors, where possible.

Color scaleThis color scale represents a general principle, most relevant for meeting spaces and public areas. The amount of color used can depend on the size of the space itself. If you are designing a large office building then you can make use of the full palette. However, smaller spaces may require less color. SEB Green must always be present and play a central role.
Please note: This is a representation of our color palette. See the Appendix at the back of this manual for full color specifications.
WoodHigh quality Oak & Ash parquet flooring are used throughout our buildings and offices. They’re strength and durability are a good choice for public areas.
StoneConsider the durability of the material and where you use it. Harder wearing materials such as limestone should be used in areas of heavy footfall.
TextilesCarpet tiles and rugs can be used to help soundproof a room as well as protect wooden flooring from chairs and furniture.
Natural lightInterior spaces with good natural light are preferable. Encourage the use of natural light by using floor-to-ceiling glass windows and atriums if possible.
Ambient lightAmbient lighting should be subtle within the building but should help to create a sophisticated overall mood.

Soft/Mood lightCreate an atmosphere with freestanding lamps and ceiling lights. Position lamps to ensure no areas are too dark, but ensure that their placement is practical and not obstructing anything in the room.
LampsConsider the height of where you place the lamps, so that everyone around the table can see each other, and so they don’t block the view of screens.

Arrangement/ CombinationsWhere possible, we like to have uneven groups of plants, either 1, (sometimes 2), 3, or 5. Their arrangement is important – they shouldn’t be in a straight line, but clustered in smaller groups to create an asymmetric expression.
Plants should preferably be placed on the floor (e.g. not on top of surfaces around the office).

The personal touchExpressive art can bring an extra layer of intellectual and emotional interest to public areas like reception, lounges, branch offices, and atriums for both customers and employees. Scale, color, and contrast help to draw the eye to these symbols of our interior design language.
Size systematicsOur signage program is an easy-to-use modular system based on the square. The square is evenly scalable from 70mm – 560mm.

Each sign creates a part of a composition defining the directions and destination in the area. The signs can be used in single or multiple compositions, mounted flat on walls, extending around corners, or as 3d cubes. Please view the following pages on how the system is created.

LayoutOur signage system is dynamic to display content and directions in the best possible way. Use the right amount of signs for the content that fits the mounting area the best. The signs can be composed in many variations and positions, so please take a minute to view some of the application examples.
MountingAlignment is key. To achieve a structured and intended look for signs place them in alignment with the actual environment. Aligned with corners, door posts, pillars, etc.
When it comes to navigating a building we need to keep accessibility in mind. That’s why real estate will be the only place we use our pictograms, eg. toilets, recycling, coffee, floors, elevators.
These are the collection of pictograms real estate can choose from.
